artifact_identity.series_role: authoritative topology, path-contract, and ownership reality map for later repo-structure discovery, design, migration-planning, and decision prompts; downstream of stronger canon, the Omega0 constraint packet, audit evidence, and live implementation evidence
artifact_identity.replacement_target: later explicit repo-structure follow-up after approved topology design or new playable-baseline evidence

ownership_class_definitions.label: authoritative / canonical root
ownership_class_definitions.meaning: A root that currently functions as the safest canonical owner of a repo-level domain of truth or behavior.

ownership_class_definitions.label: authoritative but bounded-domain root
ownership_class_definitions.meaning: A root that is authoritative for a specific implementation, data, or policy area, but is not a whole-repo owner.

ownership_class_definitions.label: derived / generated root
ownership_class_definitions.meaning: A root whose contents are produced, mirrored, materialized, or report-only rather than authored canon.

ownership_class_definitions.label: mixed / ambiguous root
ownership_class_definitions.meaning: A root that contains real value but mixes roles, hosts overlapping responsibilities, or should not be treated as a clean owner without more design work.

ownership_class_definitions.label: transitional / legacy root
ownership_class_definitions.meaning: A root that still participates in live flows or compatibility but is explicitly not the settled long-term owner.

ownership_class_definitions.label: wrapper-only root
ownership_class_definitions.meaning: A root that exposes launch, install, or operator surfaces but is currently thinner than the stronger implementation it wraps.

ownership_class_definitions.label: support-data root
ownership_class_definitions.meaning: A data root that is authoritative for a bounded machine-readable payload family, but should not be mistaken for doctrine or for a broader code owner.

topology_ledger.path: engine/, game/, and major authored domain module roots
topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Hosts the bulk of authored simulation, runtime, and domain logic outside the launcher/setup/session shell layer.
owns: Primary authored gameplay and engine implementation bodies
Primary simulation and domain-module implementation bodies
dependencies_or_path_couplings: Compiled through the verify preset family
Consumed by client, server, and test surfaces

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Repo-rooted operator shell substrate for commands, IPC, logging, supervisor behavior, and virtual path resolution.
owns: Supervision helpers used by repo-local launcher, setup, and server surfaces
does_not_own: Core client or server product logic

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Owns the client binary and client-side presentation, input, local-server bridge, and application wiring.
Orchestrates repo-local singleplayer by creating or reusing session data, spawning the Python server, and attaching loopback transport.
owns: Local-only client to server orchestration for the current baseline path

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Owns authoritative runtime, persistence, shard, and server-side net integration.
Compiled launcher root with CLI, core, GUI, include, and TUI surfaces.
does_not_own: The most complete launcher behavior path
dependencies_or_path_couplings: Current stronger shell path is under tools/launcher/

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Compiled setup root with CLI, core, GUI, include, packages, and TUI surfaces.
dependencies_or_path_couplings: Current stronger repo-local flow is tools/setup/setup_cli.py

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Release manifest, component graph, install-profile, and update resolution logic.
owns: Store and dist-oriented install payload resolution

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Trust verification and root/policy registry resolution.
Thin runtime helper root, currently dominated by process-spawn support.
owns: Bounded helper responsibilities such as server process launch specifications

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Generic network policy, transport, testing, and anti-cheat substrate.
owns: Generic transport-layer substrate consumed by server-side runtime
does_not_own: The loopback transport implementation under server/net/

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Owns the validation engine and canonical report-writing logic.
Umbrella tooling root containing canonical baseline-critical tool subroots plus many unrelated or broader admin, audit, dist, and review tools.
does_not_own: A single clean semantic or product boundary
dependencies_or_path_couplings: Many repo-root hints and relative-path contracts

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Hosts the tool entrypoints that currently define the live build, validation, session, and transitional runtime shell flows.
does_not_own: The compiled launcher and setup roots
The final settled public playtest API

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Canonical runtime packaging, activation, compatibility, distribution-descriptor, and pack-layout root.
does_not_own: All authored pack-content declarations under data/packs/

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Authored pack-content and declaration root that remains authoritative within its bounded scope while still being transitional for single-root convergence.
does_not_own: The settled single runtime packaging root for future convergence

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Authored profile root, currently dominated by profiles/bundles/.
owns: Authored profile bundle declarations used by runtime and baseline tooling
does_not_own: Generated installed profile copies under store/

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Authored pack-lock root for baseline and runtime configuration.
owns: Authored lock declarations such as pack_lock.mvp_default.json

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Authored session-template support-data root.
owns: Session template payloads such as session.mvp_default

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Machine-readable registry root consumed by AppShell, release, trust, validation, and parts of runtime and session tooling.
owns: Registry payloads for commands, panels, virtual roots, trust, install, component graphs, validation suites, session defaults, and bundle profiles

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Authoritative semantic and contract-law root.
Validator-facing JSON schema and example projection tree.
owns: Examples used by tooling and validation consumers

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Doctrine, planning, audit, repo-series, xstack closure, and semantic-specification root cluster.
does_not_own: Live implementation behavior where code disagrees

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Machine-readable mirrors and evidence payloads for planning, audit, and xstack packets.

topology_ledger.path: repo/ plus root-level control files such as AGENTS.md, .agentignore, and CMakePresets.json
topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Repo policy, release policy, canon-state markers, and root-level execution controls.
Assembled distribution tree and install-style payload staging root.
dependencies_or_path_couplings: AppShell install and store search roots

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Runtime materialization root for session saves and bootable session specs.
owns: Runtime artifacts for the current repo-local baseline

topology_ledger.actual_purpose: Generated work roots, build outputs, caches, reports, and transient artifact materialization.

repo_root_assumptions.statement: Most Python tool entrypoints under tools/ compute repo root with ../.. from the script directory. This is live in tools/launcher/launch.py, tools/setup/setup_cli.py, and tools/validation/tool_run_validation.py.
Nested SessionX modules under tools/xstack/ use deeper relative repo-root math and expect to resolve generated build outputs and save directories from repo root.
server/server_main.py computes REPO_ROOT_HINT by walking two levels up from server/, which escapes the repo root and is explicitly a live blocker.

entrypoint_path_assumptions.statement: The compiled verify lane expects CMakePresets.json to configure and build into out/build/vs2026/<preset>/, with the audited canonical tree centered on out/build/vs2026/verify/.
The verify build path produces the client and server binaries used by the audited compiled lane, even though the current repo-local shell path leans more heavily on Python/AppShell surfaces.

session_save_root_assumptions.statement: tools/xstack/session_boot.py still documents and expects boot specs at saves/<save_id>/session_spec.json.
tools/xstack/sessionx/runner.py still resolves save_dir = os.path.join(repo_root, "saves", save_id), so alternate save roots are not honored end to end.
client/local_server/local_server_controller.py and related local-loopback code assume repo-local save materialization and emit diagnostics under build/client/local_singleplayer/<save_id>/diag/.

profile_bundle_lock_template_location_as.statement: The authored baseline asset trio is physically located at profiles/bundles/bundle.mvp_default.json, locks/pack_lock.mvp_default.json, and data/session_templates/session.mvp_default.json.

The authored baseline asset trio coexists with registry-driven defaults in data/registries/bundle_profiles.json and a SessionX default bundle id of bundle.base.lab from tools/xstack/registry_compile/constants.py.
SessionX create and runner paths currently depend on build/registries/ and build/lockfile.json as generated intermediates.
appshell/paths/virtual_paths.py and data/registries/virtual_root_registry.json encode default search locations for packs, profiles, locks, saves, runtime, and store/install-style fallbacks.

manifest_registry_location_assumptions.statement: The repo-local launcher/setup shells and release logic expect release manifests at manifests/release_manifest.json, with the repo-local install/discovery path commonly resolving via dist/manifests/release_manifest.json.
Release/update logic also expects installed or projected payloads such as store/profiles/bundles/bundle.mvp_default.json and store/locks/pack_lock.mvp_default.json.

hard_coded_or_brittle_relative_expectati.statement: schema/ remains canonical semantic law while schemas/ remains the validator-facing projection and example tree.

packs/ remains canonical for runtime packaging, activation, compatibility, and distribution-descriptor scope, while data/packs/ remains authoritative within authored pack-content and declaration scope.

entrypoint_dependency_map.entrypoint: cmake --preset verify; cmake --build --preset verify; ctest --preset verify
entrypoint_dependency_map.notes: Canonical compiled build and test lane frozen by Omega0 and the audit packet.
Canonical TestX harness lane retained by Omega0.
Current boot surface with unresolved alternate-save-root coupling.
Transitional shell, not yet the hardened public playtest path.

canonical_vs_derived_location_map.current_role: Runtime packaging, activation, compatibility, and distribution descriptor root
canonical_vs_derived_location_map.why: Governance and pack docs treat it as canonical for runtime packaging scope.
Still authoritative for authored pack-content scope, but not the settled single-root future.
Current baseline assets live here and AppShell resolves profiles here.
Baseline lock lives here; generated and store copies exist elsewhere.
Current baseline template lives here and tooling references it directly.

canonical_vs_derived_location_map.current_role: Machine-readable runtime, shell, release, trust, and validation registries
canonical_vs_derived_location_map.why: Live code reads these registries directly.
SessionX create and runner depend on them, but they are outputs, not authored canon.
Current SessionX path still expects it.
Governance explicitly assigns canonical status here.
Useful and consumed, but not canonical law.
High-value evidence, but not implementation truth when code differs.

canonical_vs_derived_location_map.current_role: XStack closure and retained or deferred contracts
canonical_vs_derived_location_map.why: XStack/AIDE closure is frozen and constraining for this series.
Launcher, setup, and AppShell discover it, but it is not authored canon.
Not authored truth, but the current baseline path assumes it.

overlap_duplication_ambiguity_findings.statement: tools/ is top-level prominent but not a clean owner. Later prompts must reason at the subroot level, not by assuming the umbrella root is canonical.

launcher/ and setup/ look like natural product owners because they are first-class compiled roots, but the stronger repo-local operator truth currently sits in tools/launcher/launch.py and tools/setup/setup_cli.py.
runtime/ and validation/ are real roots, but both are thinner than the operational flows that depend on them. Later topology changes cannot assume their small size means low coupling.
net/ and server/net/ are separate roots with operationally entangled responsibilities.
schema/ and schemas/ remain an active canonical-versus-projection split.
packs/ and data/packs/ remain an active runtime-packaging-versus-authored-pack-content split.

profiles/bundles/, locks/, data/session_templates/, and data/registries/ are all live inputs, but the current default-bundle story is split between authored MVP assets and registry-compiled bundle.base.lab.
dist/ is generated, but it is still on the operational path for launcher/setup discovery.
saves/ is not authored canon, but it is a baseline-critical operational contract today.

later_prompts_may_redesign_conceptually: The eventual top-level grouping or clustering model
How to present or document ownership more clearly
How to reduce ambiguity between wrapper roots and stronger implementation roots
How to phase future convergence between authored and generated intermediates
How to eventually normalize the bundle, profile, lock, and template default story
later_prompts_must_preserve_current_real: appshell/ is the current canonical repo-local shell and bootstrap owner
The strongest launcher and setup flows are currently the Python/AppShell shells under tools/
The playable-baseline path is repo-local, session-based, and loopback-authoritative
server/server_main.py repo-root math is still blocked
SessionX runner still hardcodes repo-local saves/<save_id>
Authored MVP assets and generated registry and lockfile intermediates currently coexist
schema/ versus schemas/ and packs/ versus data/packs/ are still active ownership splits
dist/ is derived but operationally consumed
later_prompts_must_not_ignore_even_if_mo: profiles/, locks/, data/session_templates/, and data/registries/ physical locations as current live inputs

prohibited_repo_topology_misreadings: Assuming top-level prominence means canonical ownership
Treating tools/ as a clean owner without drilling into the actual subroot entrypoints
Treating launcher/ or setup/ native wrappers as canonical just because they look cleaner than the Python shells
Collapsing schema/ and schemas/ into one class before the ownership split is designed explicitly
Collapsing packs/ and data/packs/ into one class before the authored-versus-runtime split is designed explicitly
Ignoring generated intermediates like build/registries/, build/lockfile.json, or dist/ because they are non-canonical, even though live flows still depend on them
Moving saves/ assumptions too early while the baseline path still hardcodes them
Using stale docs or root names as topology truth when live implementation evidence shows otherwise
Presenting the current tree as already normalized when the bundle-default seam, wrapper-versus-shell seam, and save-root coupling remain unresolved
downstream_prompts_enabled: Repo-structure coupling and drift analysis grounded in current ownership and path contracts
Candidate topology comparison against live baseline-critical roots and path dependencies
Migration sequencing and shim-strategy planning after coupling risks are mapped
